rationale: Unapproved spec metadata must block TDD before tests are written.
Approved version and hash must certify the active spec, not a stale predecessor.
Hash algorithm mismatch must be caught even when both values look plausible.
Conflicting task and ADR module boundaries make test scope uncertifiable.
Superseded ADR references must break the certification chain even when the active ADR looks approved.
Approval metadata must not override inline NEEDS CLARIFICATION blockers.
Certification records must include spec ID, version, and hash.
Exact hash enforcement must reject wildcard certification values.
Typo-only non-behavioral changes should not trigger false refusal or needless rewrite.

agent_dimension: 2. Requirement Coverage, Outcome Matrix & Implicit NFRs
rationale: Outcome matrices must describe observable state and input outcomes rather than implementation branches.

agent_dimension: 2. Requirement Coverage, Outcome Matrix & Implicit NFRs
rationale: Coverage gaps need risk levels and security/session gaps must not be demoted beneath cosmetic work.

agent_dimension: 2. Requirement Coverage, Outcome Matrix & Implicit NFRs
rationale: Pure invariants should be tested with fast unit tests before slow acceptance coverage.

agent_dimension: 2. Requirement Coverage, Outcome Matrix & Implicit NFRs
rationale: ADR capacity constraints can imply testable NFR gaps absent from explicit ACs.

agent_dimension: 2. Requirement Coverage, Outcome Matrix & Implicit NFRs
rationale: Outcome matrix expectations must not contradict the spec's observable behavior.

agent_dimension: 2. Requirement Coverage, Outcome Matrix & Implicit NFRs
rationale: Boundary coverage must include empty, maximum, and max-plus-one cases, not only normal non-empty inputs.

agent_dimension: 2. Requirement Coverage, Outcome Matrix & Implicit NFRs
rationale: Obvious uncovered acceptance criteria with spec references must produce tests or explicit risk-ranked gaps.

agent_dimension: 2. Requirement Coverage, Outcome Matrix & Implicit NFRs
rationale: React component changes require render, interaction, accessibility, and edge-case tests, not partial stale coverage.

agent_dimension: 2. Requirement Coverage, Outcome Matrix & Implicit NFRs
rationale: Deprecated out-of-scope behavior should not receive tests merely to inflate coverage.
Tests should assert public observable behavior instead of private state.
Validation ranges and invariants require property-based tests when examples are insufficient.
Conflicting ADR contract approaches require escalation or selected-approach fidelity, not guessing.
Async tests must avoid fixed sleeps and production delay helpers.
No-spec uncovered routes should be routed as implementation drift instead of tested into legitimacy.
React component coverage must include render, interaction, accessibility, and edge cases.
ADR-defined API contracts with zero tests are critical TDD omissions.
Gap-fill updates must preserve the original certified spec hash.
Snapshot-heavy contract tests are not defects when the ADR explicitly selects and justifies that approach.
